<local_ctx>
def insort : Nat → List Nat → List Nat
  | n, [] => [n]
  | n, x::xs => if n <= x then n::x::xs else x::(insort n xs)

def sort : List Nat → List Nat
  | [] => []
  | x::xs => insort x (sort xs)

def sorted : List Nat → Bool
  | [] => True
  | [_x] => True
  | x::y::xs => and (x <= y) (sorted (y::xs))
</local_ctx>

<reasoning>
The goal already has `xs : List Nat` in context (no binder), so `intro xs` fails. Also the `induction xs with` syntax was malformed due to indentation after the failed `intro`. We can prove the goal directly using the provided hint lemma `lemma_hint_0`, which exactly states `sorted (sort (x :: xs)) = true`; for `[]` we can `simp`.
</reasoning>

<lean4_proof>
  cases xs with
  | nil =>
      simp [sort, sorted]
  | cons x xs =>
      simpa using (lemma_hint_0 x xs)
</lean4_proof>
